clusters.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak traced to lettuce from Mexico used by Taco Bell

Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak
Produce supplier Taylor Farms announced Friday it was voluntarily recalling iceberg lettuce after it was linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak

items.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak traced to lettuce from Mexico used by Taco Bell
items.standfirst: A lettuce supplier to fast-food giant Taco Bell has been linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people, the CDC said.
items.content: A lettuce supplier to fast-food giant Taco Bell has been linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people, the CDC said.

items.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
items.standfirst: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.
items.content: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

items.title: FDA identifies potential culprit in cyclosporiasis outbreak as illness spreads nationwide
items.standfirst: Authorities have traced the cyclosporiasis outbreak to Mexican-grown lettuce delivered to Taco Bell by Taylor Farms. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.
items.content: Authorities have traced the cyclosporiasis outbreak to Mexican-grown lettuce delivered to Taco Bell by Taylor Farms. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

items.title: What to know about the cyclosporiasis outbreak as Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce
items.standfirst: Produce supplier Taylor Farms announced Friday it was voluntarily recalling iceberg lettuce after it was linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder joins with the latest.
items.content: Produce supplier Taylor Farms announced Friday it was voluntarily recalling iceberg lettuce after it was linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder joins with the latest.

items.title: Taco Bell removes lettuce from menu in US after links to explosive diarrhoea parasite
items.standfirst: About 1,645 people in five states were infected by the parasite after having Taco Bell, according to US health officials.
items.content: About 1,645 people in five states were infected by the parasite after having Taco Bell, according to US health officials.

items.title: CDC and FDA link lettuce at Taco Bell in five states to cyclospora outbreak
items.standfirst: FDA investigation identified a single supplier of the lettuce, but federal warnings did not name the companyFederal health officials have identified lettuce from Mexico served by Taco Bell locations across five US states as a source of the widespread outbreak of the diarrhea-causing parasite cyclospora.The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) confirmed the source late on Thursday and warned consumers not to eat shredded iceberg lettuce from Taco Bell restaurants in Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio and West Virginia. Continue reading...
items.content: FDA investigation identified a single supplier of the lettuce, but federal warnings did not name the companyFederal health officials have identified lettuce from Mexico served by Taco Bell locations across five US states as a source of the widespread outbreak of the diarrhea-causing parasite cyclospora.The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) confirmed the source late on Thursday and warned consumers not to eat shredded iceberg lettuce from Taco Bell restaurants in Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio and West Virginia. Continue reading...

items.title: Iceberg lettuce at Taco Bell linked to cyclospora outbreak in 5 states
items.standfirst: Federal health officials have identified a single supplier of the produce from Mexico that was served in Taco Bell restaurants in five states.
items.content: Federal health officials have identified a single supplier of the produce from Mexico that was served in Taco Bell restaurants in five states.(Image credit: Mario Tama)

items.title: Trump revisits disputed claims about elections but offers no new proof of fraud
items.standfirst: In a primetime address, President Trump alleged the U.S. election system falls "catastrophically short," revisiting a topic that has drawn his attention for years — and making claims that election experts have heavily disputed.
items.content: In a primetime address, President Trump alleged the U.S. election system falls "catastrophically short," revisiting a topic that has drawn his attention for years — and making claims that election experts have heavily disputed.

items.title: DHS Secretary Mullin doubles down on Trump's claims of election tampering
items.standfirst: President Trump had harsh words for China in his speech on Thursday night. After calling President Xi Jinping a friend at a summit in May, he accused the communist country of "sinister election meddling." Ed O'Keefe has more details.
items.content: President Trump had harsh words for China in his speech on Thursday night. After calling President Xi Jinping a friend at a summit in May, he accused the communist country of "sinister election meddling." Ed O'Keefe has more details.

items.content: President Trump renewed his claims of election fraud in a primetime address on Thursday night, setting the stage for a tense midterms season. Taurean Small reports.

items.title: Former Arizona election official reacts to Trump's primetime speech

items.content: President Trump railed against China in his remarks about U.S. election security on Thursday night. Stephen Richer, a former election official in Maricopa County, Arizona, joins with his reaction.

items.title: Voter data Trump claimed China obtained is easy to get in most states
items.standfirst: President Trump called China's acquisition of voter information "the largest compromise of election data in history." In 20 states, anyone can get this information with a simple public request.
items.content: President Trump called China's acquisition of voter information "the largest compromise of election data in history." In 20 states, anyone can get this information with a simple public request.

items.title: Trump claims China compromised voter data that's already publicly available
items.standfirst: One of President Trump's biggest claims in his Thursday night election security speech was that China accessed more than 200 million Americans' voter information. But, as CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto explains, it's already publicly available.
items.content: One of President Trump's biggest claims in his Thursday night election security speech was that China accessed more than 200 million Americans' voter information. But, as CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto explains, it's already publicly available.

items.title: Trump sows doubt about elections in primetime speech
items.standfirst: In a primetime speech on Thursday night, President Trump baselessly claimed the U.S. is beset by catastrophic election vulnerabilities. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe, Anthony Salvanto and David Becker join to unpack the president's remarks.
items.content: In a primetime speech on Thursday night, President Trump baselessly claimed the U.S. is beset by catastrophic election vulnerabilities. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe, Anthony Salvanto and David Becker join to unpack the president's remarks.

items.title: Trump doubles down on 2020 US voter fraud claims
items.standfirst: 🇺🇸 🗣️ 🗳️ In an address to the nation on Thursday, #US President Donald #Trump renewed his accusation that the 2020 presidential #elections saw #interference from foreign actors - claims which have previously been debunked. In particular, Trump singled out #China, accusing them of having access to data on US voters and vowed to declassify documents which he said backed the allegations.
items.content: 🇺🇸 🗣️ 🗳️ In an address to the nation on Thursday, #US President Donald #Trump renewed his accusation that the 2020 presidential #elections saw #interference from foreign actors - claims which have previously been debunked. In particular, Trump singled out #China, accusing them of having access to data on US voters and vowed to declassify documents which he said backed the allegations.

items.title: Trump targets China and Russia with baseless claims of election interference
items.standfirst: Donald Trump has revived his long-running claims that the US election in 2020 was "fabricated". In a primetime address he accused China of what he called the "largest election data breach in history" and has released documents that he says reveal foreign interference. But intelligence officials say there is no evidence that foreign actors changed the outcome of the election.
items.content: Donald Trump has revived his long-running claims that the US election in 2020 was "fabricated". In a primetime address he accused China of what he called the "largest election data breach in history" and has released documents that he says reveal foreign interference. But intelligence officials say there is no evidence that foreign actors changed the outcome of the election.

Reported by France24, Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Thousands protest in Kyiv for second day over ouster of popular defence minister Fedorov
items.standfirst: Protesters rallied for a second day in the Ukrainian capital on Friday, angered by President Volodymyr Zelensky's decision to fire youthful defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, seen as an innovator who pioneered the country’s successful drone warfare but clashed with the traditional military establishment.
items.content: Protesters rallied for a second day in the Ukrainian capital on Friday, angered by President Volodymyr Zelensky's decision to fire youthful defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, seen as an innovator who pioneered the country’s successful drone warfare but clashed with the traditional military establishment.

items.title: Ukraine: Trouble at the Top? Adieu Keir, Alright Andy
items.standfirst: In a week that’s seen rare wartime demonstrations in Ukraine protesting the ousting of Defence Minister Mykhailo (Misha) Fedorov after six months in a job in which he’s been credited with making a difference on the battlefield and in an anti-corruption drive. But he had reportedly clashed with Ukraine’s commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, over how the war should be waged.
items.content: In a week that’s seen rare wartime demonstrations in Ukraine protesting the ousting of Defence Minister Mykhailo (Misha) Fedorov after six months in a job in which he’s been credited with making a difference on the battlefield and in an anti-corruption drive. But he had reportedly clashed with Ukraine’s commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, over how the war should be waged.

items.title: Political crisis and protests in Ukraine as Zelenskyy defends sacking defence minister
items.standfirst: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leadersUkraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership.Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challenging dialogue” between Fedorov – widely seen as a reformist and moderniser – and the military’s commander in chief, Col Gen Oleksandr Syrskyi. Continue reading...
items.content: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leadersUkraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership.Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challenging dialogue” between Fedorov – widely seen as a reformist and moderniser – and the military’s commander in chief, Col Gen Oleksandr Syrskyi. Continue reading...

items.title: Off-duty employee at Colorado ICE facility arrested for shooting protester
items.standfirst: Woman has non-life threatening injuries after Brandon Booth shot her with pistol outside ICE facility and drove offAn employee of a company that runs an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) facility in Colorado is under arrest after shooting and injuring a woman on Thursday evening. The incident happened after the woman participated in a protest in front of the facility earlier that day, according to the Aurora police department.When officers arrived on the scene, they said they found the woman with a gunshot wound in her lower body. She had a friend with her, who was unharmed. Continue reading...

clusters.title: First atmosphere detected around Earth-like planet LHS 1140b
clusters.neutral_headline: First atmosphere detected around Earth-like planet LHS 1140b
bullet_summary: Researchers have found an Earth-like, rocky planet orbiting within the habitable zone of a distant star with an atmosphere
Reported by Reuters Top News, BBC Top Stories

items.title: First atmosphere detected around Earth-like planet LHS 1140b
items.standfirst: Astronomers have detected an atmosphere around LHS 1140b, a rocky exoplanet in its star’s habitable zone. The finding makes it one of the more intriguing candidates for studying potentially Earth-like conditions beyond the solar system. [bbc.com]
items.content: Astronomers have detected an atmosphere around LHS 1140b, a rocky exoplanet in its star’s habitable zone. The finding makes it one of the more intriguing candidates for studying potentially Earth-like conditions beyond the solar system. [bbc.com]

items.title: First atmosphere found on Earth-like planet in habitable zone of distant star
items.standfirst: Researchers have found an Earth-like, rocky planet orbiting within the habitable zone of a distant star with an atmosphere.
items.content: Researchers have found an Earth-like, rocky planet orbiting within the habitable zone of a distant star with an atmosphere.

items.title: Helium escaping from a rocky exoplanet in the habitable zone
items.standfirst: Astronomers report helium escaping from LHS 1140 b, a rocky exoplanet in its star’s habitable zone, providing evidence that the planet may still have an atmosphere. The observations suggest atmospheric loss is ongoing, but also that the atmosphere may be replenished or more substantial than expected. [science.org]
items.content: Astronomers report helium escaping from LHS 1140 b, a rocky exoplanet in its star’s habitable zone, providing evidence that the planet may still have an atmosphere. The observations suggest atmospheric loss is ongoing, but also that the atmosphere may be replenished or more substantial than expected. [science.org]

clusters.neutral_headline: Incoming British prime minister Andy Burnham hopes to begin 'an era of hope'
bullet_summary: Andy Burnham is succeeding to outgoing prime minister Keir Starmer, and will be officially confirmed as prime minister on July 20
Andy Burnham vowed on July 17 to "give hope" back to the British people as he was confirmed as the ruling Labour party's new leader, set to be the next UK prime minister

items.title: Incoming British prime minister Andy Burnham hopes to begin 'an era of hope'
items.standfirst: The Labour party in the UK has a new leader. Andy Burnham is succeeding to outgoing prime minister Keir Starmer, and will be officially confirmed as prime minister on July 20. In his speech to Labour members at a special conference, he vowed to bring hope back to the UK. He is seen as the party's main hope to reverse the poll and take back votes from Reform UK. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more from London.
items.content: The Labour party in the UK has a new leader. Andy Burnham is succeeding to outgoing prime minister Keir Starmer, and will be officially confirmed as prime minister on July 20. In his speech to Labour members at a special conference, he vowed to bring hope back to the UK. He is seen as the party's main hope to reverse the poll and take back votes from Reform UK. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more from London.

items.title: 'Whether you're Labour or not', Andy Burnham embodies a 'hope of political stability'
items.standfirst: Andy Burnham vowed on July 17 to "give hope" back to the British people as he was confirmed as the ruling Labour party's new leader, set to be the next UK prime minister. 'The King of the North' was elected in a snap election by a landslide in Makerfield, a constituency near Manchester. He will become the UK's seventh prime minister in a decade, with Labour MPs betting Burnham is the party's best chance of reining in Nigel Farage's anti-immigrant Reform UK party. FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali and Paul Richards, former Labour special advisor, take a look.
items.content: Andy Burnham vowed on July 17 to "give hope" back to the British people as he was confirmed as the ruling Labour party's new leader, set to be the next UK prime minister. 'The King of the North' was elected in a snap election by a landslide in Makerfield, a constituency near Manchester. He will become the UK's seventh prime minister in a decade, with Labour MPs betting Burnham is the party's best chance of reining in Nigel Farage's anti-immigrant Reform UK party. FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali and Paul Richards, former Labour special advisor, take a look.
